segments.text: Elihu continued speaking.
Be patient with me just a little while longer and let me explain. I still have something to say on God's behalf.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I will share my extensive knowledge,  and I will prove my Creator is in the right.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I assure you that what I'm saying are not lies, for I am a man whose knowledge is of the highest order.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God is mighty, but doesn't despise anyone; he is powerful in strength and understanding.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He does not keep the wicked alive, but gives justice to the oppressed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He always pays attention to those who do right, and places them on thrones with kings, honoring them eternally.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If they are bound in chains, tied down by ropes of suffering,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: then he explains to them what they've done—their arrogant sins.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He makes them pay attention and orders them to stop sinning.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If they listen and do what God says they will live out their lives in happiness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But if they do not listen they will die a violent death,  ignorant of God.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Those who reject God hold on to their bitterness. Even when he disciplines them they do not cry out to him for help.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They die in their youth; their lives end among the male temple prostitutes.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Through suffering God saves those who suffer; he gets their attention through their troubles.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God is trying to rescue you from the jaws of trouble to a place of freedom and safety,  filling your table full of the very best food.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But you are preoccupied with the fate of the wicked; judgment and justice fill your mind.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But be careful that your anger doesn't seduce you into mockery; and don't let the size of the ‘bribe’ lead you into sin.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Will your cry for help sustain you when troubles come?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Do not long for the night when people are suddenly taken away.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Watch out that you don't turn to evil! For it's because of this that you are being tested through suffering.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Look how much power God has! What teacher is like him?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Who has instructed him what to do? Who can say to him ‘You have done wrong’?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Instead you should praise him for what he has done, as people have done in song.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Everyone has seen God's creation, though only from a distance.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: See how great God is—more than we can understand! No one can count his years.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He draws up the water and distils it into the dew and the rain.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The clouds pour down rain, falling plentifully on humankind.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Can anyone understand how the clouds spread out, or how thunder roars from where he lives?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: See how he scatters lightning around him, and covers the depths of the sea in darkness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: By these actions he rules the people, he provides abundant food.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He holds lightning in his hands and commands where it should strike.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Thunder declares his presence—even cattle know when a storm is coming.
segments.speaker: Unknown
